title: Canonical sqrt(-1) mod pq and its Hensel lift to (pq)^2, reduced mod 1000

identity.short_name: Canonical sqrt(-1) mod pq and its Hensel lift to (pq)^2, reduced mod 1000

problem_spec.summary: For primes p,q \equiv 1 (mod 4), classify the square roots of -1 modulo N=pq via CRT, choose the least positive u, then uniquely lift u to a square root v modulo N^2 using Hensel's lemma; output v mod 1000.
givens: p=13 and q=17 are primes
N=pq
u is the least positive integer such that u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N)
v is the least positive integer such that v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) and v \equiv u (mod N)
A is the unique integer with 0 \leq A < 1000 and A \equiv v (mod 1000)
ask: Determine A.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: ensures -1 is a quadratic residue mod p

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).domain: odd prime with q \equiv 1 (mod 4), q \neq p
parameter(s).purpose: second prime factor; CRT produces 4 roots modulo N=pq

parameter(s).name: canonical_choice_u
parameter(s).purpose: selects a unique representative u among the CRT roots mod N

parameter(s).name: canonical_choice_v
parameter(s).purpose: selects the unique Hensel lift v modulo N^2 with v \equiv u (mod N), then picks its least positive representative

parameter(s).name: output_modulus
parameter(s).purpose: normalization modulus for the final answer

invariant(s).name: roots_exist_and_count
invariant(s).statement: Since p \equiv q \equiv 1 (mod 4), x^2 \equiv -1 is solvable modulo p and q, giving 2 roots each; CRT implies exactly 4 roots modulo N=pq.

invariant(s).name: invertible_derivative_condition
invariant(s).statement: From u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N) it follows gcd(u,N)=1, hence gcd(2u,N)=1 because N is odd.

invariant(s).name: hensel_linearization_x2_plus_1
invariant(s).statement: Writing v=u+tN, the condition v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) is equivalent to the linear congruence m+2ut \equiv 0 (mod N), where m=(u^2+1)/N. Since gcd(2u,N)=1, the solution t (mod N) is unique.

essential_assumption(s): p and q are odd primes with p \equiv q \equiv 1 (mod 4) so that -1 has square roots modulo each prime.
The congruence condition v \equiv u (mod N) is included to select a unique lift modulo N^2.
uniqueness_certificate(s): CRT produces exactly four roots of x^2 \equiv -1 (mod N); choosing the least positive fixes u uniquely.
For f(x)=x^2+1, if gcd(f'(u),N)=gcd(2u,N)=1 then Hensel's lemma gives a unique lift v (mod N^2) with v \equiv u (mod N) and v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2); choosing the least positive fixes v.
Reduction modulo 1000 gives a unique A in {0,...,999} with A \equiv v (mod 1000).

technique(s).name: quadratic_residue_step
technique(s).purpose: establish existence of square roots of -1 and find explicit roots in small moduli
outputs: explicit residue classes for roots of x^2 \equiv -1

technique(s).name: CRT_combination
technique(s).purpose: combine local roots into the complete set of roots modulo N=pq
outputs: complete list of roots modulo N

technique(s).name: minimality_argument
technique(s).purpose: select least positive u and least positive v from their residue classes

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting
inputs: u with u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N) and gcd(2u,N)=1
technique(s).purpose: lift u uniquely to v modulo N^2 by linearizing (u+tN)^2+1
outputs: linear congruence for t and a unique lifted residue class v (mod N^2)

technique(s).name: modular_inverse
technique(s).purpose: solve for t modulo N using an explicit inverse of 2u

technique(s).name: canonical_residue_normalization
technique(s).purpose: reduce v to the unique A in {0,...,999} with A \equiv v (mod 1000)

theorem(s).name: Euler_criterion
theorem(s).role: shows (-1/r)=1 for primes r \equiv 1 (mod 4), ensuring solvability of x^2 \equiv -1 (mod r)

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: gives existence and uniqueness of simultaneous solutions modulo pq from solutions modulo p and q

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma
theorem(s).role: provides existence and uniqueness of a lift from modulus N to N^2 when f'(u) is invertible modulo N

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Linearization of congruences: solve a higher-modulus congruence by correcting a lower-modulus solution via an invertible derivative.

critical_trick(s): CRT case-split to enumerate all roots of x^2 \equiv -1 (mod pq) and choose the least positive.
Hensel-lift for f(x)=x^2+1 using v=u+tN, turning v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) into a linear congruence for t.
Normalize the uniquely determined lift v by reduction mod 1000.

library(s).reason: deterministic enumeration of variable renamings/permutations for novelty auditing

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks consistent with the intended solution path:
- Enumerate roots of x^2 \equiv -1 modulo p and q and CRT-combine to list all roots modulo N; confirm the least positive u.
- Compute the Hensel lift parameter t from the linear congruence m+2ut \equiv 0 (mod N), build v=u+tN, and verify v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) and v \equiv u (mod N).
- Reduce v modulo 1000 to obtain A.
Preflight attempts:
Attempt A: naive guess v=u gives A_guess=u mod 1000, which does not match the verified A.
Attempt B: bounded search for v \leq 2000 satisfying v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) and v \equiv u (mod N) finds no solution.
Attempt C: using only the relaxed constraint v^2 \equiv -1 (mod N^2) (dropping v \equiv u (mod N)) leaves multiple residues for A in {0,...,999}.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independent verification route:
- Lift the chosen roots of -1 separately from mod p to mod p^2 and from mod q to mod q^2 (prime-power Hensel step),
  then CRT-combine the lifted roots modulo p^2 q^2 = N^2 to obtain v; confirm it equals the primary v.

task(s).name: novelty_equivalence_audit
task(s).reason: Canonicalize the parent vs daughter kernel constraint polynomials using sympy (expand + simplify + together + cancel + Poly in a fixed monomial order), normalize scale (clear denominators; divide by gcd of coefficients; fix sign/leading coefficient), and test equality up to variable renaming/permutation and global scaling. Reject if any transformed canonical form matches.

verification_notes: Enumerated all four CRT roots from local roots [5, 8] mod 13 and [4, 13] mod 17; the least positive global root is u=21.
The unique lift to modulus 48841 is v=4662, and v mod 1000 = 662.

title: Counting units with forced local order $p$ and global order $p^2$ via CRT, lifting, and exact-order counting

identity.short_name: Counting units with forced local order $p$ and global order $p^2$ via CRT, lifting, and exact-order counting

problem_spec.summary:
Let $p,q,r$ be distinct odd primes with $a\ge 2$, $b\ge 1$, $c\ge 1$ and assume $p\mid(q-1)$ and $p^2\mid(r-1)$.
Set $M=p^a q^b r^c$ and count units $x\pmod M$ satisfying a mixed local-global system:
$v_p(x-1)=a-1$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x)=p$ by a binomial/LTE valuation jump,
$x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$ with $v_q(x-1)=0$ selects exactly $p-1$ nontrivial $p$th roots by Hensel lifting,
and the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ forces $\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x)=p^2$ via the lcm-of-orders rule under CRT.
The $r$-component count is then $\varphi(p^2)$ in a cyclic unit group.
givens: Distinct odd primes $p,q,r$.
Integers $a\ge 2$, $b\ge 1$, $c\ge 1$.
Guards: $p\mid(q-1)$ and $p^2\mid(r-1)$.
Define $M=p^a q^b r^c$.
Define $S$ to be the set of units $x\pmod M$ such that $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$, $v_p(x-1)=a-1$, $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$, and $v_q(x-1)=0$.
ask: Let $C=|S|$. Output the unique integer $A\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ with $A\equiv C\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: Sets the target global order $p^2$ and the forced local order $p$ modulo $p^a$.

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: Ensures existence of nontrivial $p$th roots of unity modulo $q^b$.

parameter(s).name: r
parameter(s).purpose: Ensures existence of elements of exact order $p^2$ in $(\mathbb Z/r^c\mathbb Z)^\times$.

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: Depth for the exact valuation constraint $v_p(x-1)=a-1$.

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: Depth for lifting $p$th roots modulo $q^b$.

parameter(s).name: c
parameter(s).purpose: Depth for the $r$-power modulus $r^c$.

invariant(s).name: crt_factorization
invariant(s).statement:
With $M=p^a q^b r^c$ and $\gcd(p^a,q^b)=\gcd(p^a,r^c)=\gcd(q^b,r^c)=1$, CRT gives a bijection
$x\pmod M \leftrightarrow (x\pmod{p^a},x\pmod{q^b},x\pmod{r^c})$ for residues, and an isomorphism on unit groups.

invariant(s).name: order_lcm_under_crt
invariant(s).statement:
For a unit $x\pmod M$ with components $(x_p,x_q,x_r)$ under CRT,
$\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x_p),\operatorname{ord}_{q^b}(x_q),\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x_r))$.

invariant(s).name: valuation_forces_local_order_p
invariant(s).statement: If $v_p(x-1)=a-1\ge 1$, then a binomial/LTE valuation jump gives $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{p^a}$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x)=p$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_filters_nontrivial_p_roots_mod_qb
invariant(s).statement:
Under $p\mid(q-1)$, the congruence $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{q^b}$ has exactly $p$ solutions, each lifting uniquely from modulo $q$,
and exactly one satisfies $x\equiv 1\pmod q$, so imposing $v_q(x-1)=0$ leaves exactly $p-1$ solutions of order $p$.

invariant(s).name: global_order_forces_r_component
invariant(s).statement:
If $\operatorname{ord}_{p^a}(x_p)=p$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{q^b}(x_q)=p$, then the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$
forces $\operatorname{ord}_{r^c}(x_r)=p^2$ because $\operatorname{lcm}(p,p,m)=p^2$ implies $m$ is divisible by $p^2$.

essential_assumption(s): The primes are odd and pairwise distinct so CRT applies cleanly and lifting steps avoid $p=2$ pathologies.
The guards $p\mid(q-1)$ and $p^2\mid(r-1)$ ensure the local solution sets at $q^b$ and $r^c$ are nonempty.
$a\ge 2$ ensures $v_p(x-1)=a-1\ge 1$ so the valuation jump at $p$ is nontrivial.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
CRT splits residues into independent components. The $p$-adic valuation constraint forces exactly $p-1$ classes modulo $p^a$ and forces local order $p$.
The $q$-adic constraints select exactly $p-1$ nontrivial $p$th roots modulo $q^b$ by cyclic root counting and Hensel lifting.
With these local orders fixed as $p$ and $p$, the global constraint $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ forces the $r$-component to have exact order $p^2$.
The unit group modulo $r^c$ is cyclic for odd $r$, so there are exactly $\varphi(p^2)$ such elements.
Therefore $C=(p-1)(p-1)\varphi(p^2)$ is forced and the output modulo $1000$ is unique.

technique(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
inputs: Factorization $M=p^a q^b r^c$ with pairwise coprime factors
technique(s).purpose: Reduce global congruence and order conditions to local conditions and combine counts.
outputs: A bijection between $S$ and a set of compatible CRT triples

technique(s).name: p_adic_valuation_filtering
technique(s).purpose: Parametrize admissible residue classes by exact divisibility.
outputs: Finite explicit local parametrizations such as $x\equiv 1+p^{a-1}t$

technique(s).name: lifting_the_exponent
inputs: Binomial valuation jump in $(1+p^{a-1}t)^p-1$ for odd $p$
technique(s).purpose: Force $x^p\equiv 1\pmod{p^a}$ from the exact valuation at $p$.

technique(s).name: cyclic_group_root_count
inputs: Cyclicity of $(\mathbb Z/\ell\mathbb Z)^\times$ for prime $\ell$
technique(s).purpose: Count solutions to $y^k=1$ in prime-modulus unit groups.
outputs: Root counts such as exactly $\gcd(k,\ell-1)$ solutions modulo $\ell$

technique(s).name: hensel_lifting
inputs: Simple-root lifting for $f(X)=X^p-1$ from modulo $q$ to modulo $q^b$
technique(s).purpose: Count and classify $p$th roots modulo $q^b$ and separate the unique root congruent to $1$ modulo $q$.

technique(s).name: order_lcm_in_direct_product
technique(s).purpose: Translate $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=p^2$ into forced local order at the $r$-component.

technique(s).name: exact_order_counting
technique(s).purpose: Count elements of exact order $p^2$.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: Bijection of residues and unit-group decomposition modulo coprime factors.

theorem(s).name: lifting_the_exponent_lemma
theorem(s).role: Computes the $p$-adic valuation of $(1+p^{a-1}t)^p-1$ for odd $p$.

theorem(s).name: hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: Unique lifting of simple roots from modulo $q$ to modulo $q^2$ and hence to modulo $q^b$.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_prime_modulus_unit_group
theorem(s).role: For prime $\ell$, the group $(\mathbb Z/\ell\mathbb Z)^\times$ is cyclic of order $\ell-1$.

theorem(s).name: cyclic_group_exact_order_phi
theorem(s).role: In a cyclic group, the number of elements of exact order $d$ is $\varphi(d)$.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local-to-global structure: CRT converts a global constraint into local constraints plus an $\operatorname{lcm}$ coupling.

critical_trick(s): Use an exact $p$-adic valuation to force local order $p$ by a binomial/LTE valuation jump.
Use cyclic root counting modulo a prime and Hensel lifting to lift and filter $p$th roots modulo $q^b$.
Use the order $\operatorname{lcm}$ rule under CRT to force the remaining component to carry the extra factor of $p$.
Count exact order $p^2$ elements using $\varphi(p^2)$.

task(s).name: crt_enumeration_verification
task(s).reason: Enumerate the local solution sets modulo $5^4$, $11^2$, and $101$, combine them by CRT, and directly verify all defining congruences and $\operatorname{ord}_M(x)=25$ for each combined solution.

verification_notes: Direct local enumeration gives 4 classes modulo 5^4, 4 modulo 11^2, and 20 of exact order 25 modulo 101.
CRT multiplication yields C=320.

title: Half-Wilson roots lifted to $p^4$ and $q^2$ then glued by CRT

identity.short_name: Half-Wilson roots lifted to $p^4$ and $q^2$ then glued by CRT

problem_spec.summary:
Choose primes $p,q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ with $4p<q<5p$ so that $k=v_p((q-1)!)$ collapses to $k=4$.
Use the half-factorial consequence of Wilson's theorem to get canonical square roots of $-1$ modulo $p$ and $q$.
Lift uniquely to $p^4$ and $q^2$ using the simple-root case of Hensel lifting, glue by CRT, and report the glued residue modulo $1000$.
givens: Let $p$ and $q$ be distinct odd primes with $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$, $q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$, and $4p<q<5p$.
Define $k=v_p((q-1)!)$ and $M=p^k q^2$.
Define $B$ as the unique integer with $0\le B<p^k$ such that $B\equiv ((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and $B^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$.
Define $C$ as the unique integer with $0\le C<q^2$ such that $C\equiv A\pmod{q}$ and $C^2\equiv -1\pmod{q^2}$.
Define $r$ as the unique integer with $0\le r<M$ such that $r\equiv B\pmod{p^k}$ and $r\equiv C\pmod{q^2}$.
ask: Determine the unique $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ such that $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: provides the canonical seed $((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and the prime power modulus $p^4$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: provides the canonical seed $A=((q-1)/2)!\pmod{q}$ and the prime square modulus $q^2$

invariant(s).name: valuation_collapse_fixed_k_equals_4
invariant(s).statement: Under $4p<q<5p$ and $q<p^2$, Legendre's formula gives $k=v_p((q-1)!)=\lfloor (q-1)/p\rfloor=4$.

invariant(s).name: half_wilson_sqrt_minus_one_mod_primes
invariant(s).statement: If $p\equiv q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ are primes, then $((p-1)/2)!^2\equiv -1\pmod{p}$ and $A^2\equiv -1\pmod{q}$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_unique_lifts_to_p_four_and_q_square
invariant(s).statement: For $f(x)=x^2+1$, each seed is a simple root, so it lifts uniquely to $B\pmod{p^4}$ and $C\pmod{q^2}$.

invariant(s).name: CRT_gluing_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Since $\gcd(p^4,q^2)=1$, CRT gives a unique $r\pmod{p^4 q^2}$ satisfying the two congruences, and $0\le r<M$ fixes $r$ as an integer.

essential_assumption(s): The congruences $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ and $q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ ensure $-1$ is a quadratic residue modulo $p$ and $q$.
The range guard $4p<q<5p$ together with $q<p^2$ forces $k=4$ and fixes the lifting depth to $p^4$.
The simple-root condition $2a\not\equiv 0\pmod{p}$ for a seed $a$ is needed for uniqueness of Hensel lifts of $x^2+1$.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
The range guard forces $k=4$.
The seeds $((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and $A\pmod{q}$ are fixed residues, and the simple-root Hensel lift makes $B\pmod{p^4}$ and $C\pmod{q^2}$ unique.
CRT then gives a unique residue class $r\pmod{p^4 q^2}$, and the condition $0\le r<M$ fixes $r$.
The normalization $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$ with $0\le N<1000$ fixes the output uniquely.

technique(s).name: Legendre_valuation_floor_collapse
technique(s).purpose: use $v_p(n!)=\sum_{j\ge 1}\lfloor n/p^j\rfloor$ and the guard $q<p^2$ to force $k=4$

technique(s).name: half_Wilson_square_root_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: derive $((\pi-1)/2)!^2\equiv -1\pmod{\pi}$ when $\pi\equiv 1\pmod{4}$

technique(s).name: inverse_pairing_operator
technique(s).purpose: compute $A=((q-1)/2)!\pmod{q}$ explicitly to fix the lifted root modulo $q^2$

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting_setup
inputs: $f(x)=x^2+1$ and a simple root modulo a prime
technique(s).purpose: guarantee existence and uniqueness of lifts to $p^4$ and $q^2$

technique(s).name: extended_Euclid_mod_inverse_composite
technique(s).purpose: compute $a^{-1}\pmod{m}$ when $m$ is composite such as $q^2$

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_reconstruction
technique(s).purpose: glue local solutions into $r\pmod{p^4 q^2}$
outputs: unique $r$ and hence unique normalized output $N$

technique(s).name: mod_1000_normalization
technique(s).purpose: output the unique $N\in\{0,\dots,999\}$ with $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$

theorem(s).name: Wilson_theorem
theorem(s).role: provides $(\pi-1)!\equiv -1\pmod{\pi}$

theorem(s).name: Legendre_formula_for_v_p_of_factorial
theorem(s).role: provides $v_p(n!)=\sum_{j\ge 1}\lfloor n/p^j\rfloor$

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root_lifting
theorem(s).role: provides unique lifting of a simple root to prime powers

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: provides existence and uniqueness of solutions modulo coprime moduli

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Build canonical local roots and glue them via CRT.

critical_trick(s): Use half-factorial Wilson to manufacture roots of $x^2\equiv -1$ modulo primes.
Use simple-root Hensel lifting to lift uniquely to $p^4$ and $q^2$.
Use CRT and compute only the final residue modulo $1000$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: factorials and integer arithmetic for direct checks

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: independent CRT and modular-inverse verification

task(s).name: correctness_verification
task(s).reason:
Verify:
$k=v_p((q-1)!)=4$ for the chosen instance,
$B\equiv ((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and $B^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^4}$,
$C\equiv A\pmod{q}$ and $C^2\equiv -1\pmod{q^2}$,
CRT for $r$ modulo $p^4 q^2$,
and $N=r\bmod 1000$.

verification_notes: Computed k=v_17((73-1)!)=4, with A ≡ 27 mod 73.
Hensel lifts give B=56028 modulo 83521 and C=4553 modulo 5329; CRT then gives r ≡ 427432985.

title: Half-Wilson square root and Hensel lifting glued by CRT

identity.short_name: Half-Wilson square root and Hensel lifting glued by CRT

problem_spec.summary: Choose primes p,q \equiv 1 (mod 4) in a range forcing k=v_p((q-1)!) to a fixed value; build a canonical square root of -1 modulo q via the half-factorial consequence of Wilson, lift a canonical square root of -1 to p^k via Hensel, then glue the two residues by CRT and report the result mod 1000.
givens: Let p and q be distinct odd primes with p \equiv 1 (mod 4), q \equiv 1 (mod 4), and 3p < q < 4p.
Define k = v_p((q-1)!) and M = p^k q.
Define A = ((q-1)/2)!.
Define B as the unique integer with 0 \leq B < p^k such that B \equiv ((p-1)/2)! (mod p) and B^2 \equiv -1 (mod p^k).
Define r as the unique integer with 0 \leq r < M such that r \equiv A (mod q) and r \equiv B (mod p^k).
ask: (Structure) Prove k = 3 under the range 3p < q < 4p (so q < p^2) using Legendre's formula, and prove A^2 \equiv -1 (mod q) using the half-factorial consequence of Wilson's theorem.
(p-adic lifting) Prove B exists and is unique by applying Hensel's lemma to f(x)=x^2+1 at x0 \equiv ((p-1)/2)! (mod p).
(Computation) Determine r mod 1000 (i.e., the unique integer N in {0,1,...,999} with N \equiv r (mod 1000)).

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: supplies a canonical square root of -1 mod p via ((p-1)/2)! and enables Hensel lifting to mod p^k

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).domain: odd prime with q \equiv 1 (mod 4) and 3p < q < 4p
parameter(s).purpose: supplies a canonical square root of -1 mod q via A=((q-1)/2)! and forces k=v_p((q-1)!) to equal 3

invariant(s).name: valuation_collapse_fixed_k
invariant(s).statement: Under 3p < q < 4p and p \geq 5, we have q < 4p \leq p^2, hence v_p((q-1)!) = floor((q-1)/p) = 3 by Legendre's formula.

invariant(s).name: half_wilson_sqrt_minus_one_mod_q
invariant(s).statement: If q is prime and q \equiv 1 (mod 4), then A=((q-1)/2)! satisfies A^2 \equiv -1 (mod q), obtained by pairing i and q-i inside (q-1)! and applying Wilson's theorem.

invariant(s).name: canonical_root_and_hensel_uniqueness_mod_p_pow_k
invariant(s).statement: Let f(x)=x^2+1. With x0 \equiv ((p-1)/2)! (mod p), we have f(x0) \equiv 0 (mod p) and f'(x0)=2x0 not \equiv 0 (mod p); by Hensel's lemma there is a unique B (mod p^k) with B \equiv x0 (mod p) and B^2 \equiv -1 (mod p^k).

invariant(s).name: CRT_gluing_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Since gcd(p^k,q)=1, the congruences r \equiv B (mod p^k) and r \equiv A (mod q) have a unique solution modulo M=p^k q; the representative with 0 \leq r < M is unique.

essential_assumption(s): The congruence conditions p \equiv 1 (mod 4) and q \equiv 1 (mod 4) are essential to ensure -1 is a quadratic residue modulo p and q, enabling the square-root phase.
The range guard 3p < q < 4p is essential to force q < p^2 and hence collapse Legendre's valuation sum to k=3; without it, k could vary and the lifting depth would change.
The Hensel lift is canonical because the seed root is fixed as ((p-1)/2)! (mod p) and because f'(x0) is not divisible by p, ensuring uniqueness of the lift.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Given (p,q), the valuation uniquely fixes k and hence M=p^k q. The residue A mod q is uniquely determined by A=((q-1)/2)!, and B mod p^k is uniquely determined as the Hensel lift congruent to ((p-1)/2)! mod p. Since gcd(p^k,q)=1, CRT gives a unique r mod M; imposing 0 \leq r < M makes r unique as an integer. The required output is the unique N in {0,...,999} with N \equiv r (mod 1000).

technique(s).name: coprime_moduli_invariant
inputs: prime powers p^k and prime q
technique(s).purpose: guarantee CRT gluing is unique modulo p^k q
outputs: uniqueness of the combined residue class for r

technique(s).name: Legendre_valuation_floor_collapse
technique(s).purpose: compute k exactly using Legendre's formula under q < p^2

technique(s).name: half_Wilson_square_root_mod_prime
inputs: Wilson's theorem and pairing i with q-i
technique(s).purpose: produce the congruence A^2 \equiv -1 (mod q) when q \equiv 1 (mod 4)
outputs: canonical square root of -1 modulo q as A=((q-1)/2)!

technique(s).name: factorial_mod_prime_computation
inputs: modular multiplication and grouping in Z/qZ
technique(s).purpose: compute the specific residue A mod q (fixing the sign among the two square roots)

technique(s).name: root_mod_p_from_half_factorial
technique(s).purpose: identify x0=((p-1)/2)! mod p as a seed square root of -1 mod p

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting_setup
inputs: polynomial f(x)=x^2+1 and a simple root modulo p
technique(s).purpose: certify existence and uniqueness of the lift to modulo p^k
outputs: uniqueness of B modulo p^k with B \equiv x0 (mod p)

technique(s).name: Hensel_lift_to_p2
inputs: seed root modulo p and one-step correction
technique(s).purpose: compute the unique lift to modulo p^2

technique(s).name: Hensel_lift_to_p3
inputs: root modulo p^2 and one-step correction
technique(s).purpose: compute the unique lift to modulo p^3 (the forced depth here)

technique(s).name: CRT_linear_reduction
technique(s).purpose: reduce CRT reconstruction to a single linear congruence
outputs: linear congruence in the CRT parameter t

technique(s).name: CRT_reconstruction_via_inverse
technique(s).purpose: solve the CRT linear congruence and reconstruct r explicitly

technique(s).name: closure_certificate_square_root_mod_N
technique(s).purpose: certify r^2 \equiv -1 modulo both factors and thus modulo M

technique(s).name: mod_1000_normalization
technique(s).purpose: apply the output convention N \equiv r (mod 1000)

theorem(s).name: Wilson_theorem
theorem(s).role: yields (q-1)! \equiv -1 (mod q), which implies the half-factorial square relation by pairing

theorem(s).name: Legendre_formula_for_v_p_of_factorial
theorem(s).role: computes v_p((q-1)!) and collapses to k=3 under q < p^2

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root_lifting
theorem(s).role: guarantees existence and uniqueness of the lift of a simple root modulo p to modulo p^k

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: guarantees existence and uniqueness of r modulo p^k q from the two coprime congruences

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local-to-global gluing: build canonical local solutions modulo prime and prime power, lift via p-adic uniqueness, then glue via CRT and reduce modulo 1000.

critical_trick(s): Use the half-factorial consequence of Wilson's theorem to manufacture a canonical square root of -1 modulo q as A=((q-1)/2)! when q \equiv 1 (mod 4).
Use Hensel's lemma to lift the canonical seed root x0=((p-1)/2)! (mod p) uniquely to a root B modulo p^k.
Glue the two residues by CRT, then apply the mod-1000 normalization.

library(s).reason: factorials and integer arithmetic (gcd), plus modular reduction

library(s).name: itertools

library(s).name: sympy

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks aligned with the intended path:
- Recompute k=v_p((q-1)!) via Legendre's floor-sum and confirm k=3 and p^k=2197.
- Verify A=((q-1)/2)! mod q and check A^2 \equiv -1 (mod q) for q\equiv1 (mod 4).
- Compute B via Hensel lifting and verify B \equiv ((p-1)/2)! (mod p) and B^2 \equiv -1 (mod p^k).
- Reconstruct r via CRT and verify r \equiv A (mod q) and r \equiv B (mod p^k), then record r mod 1000.
Preflight attempts (cheap solvers that must fail):
- "Attempt A: naive guess = ignore the lifting depth and glue only modulo p (i.e., work modulo pq); compute the resulting residue mod 1000 and confirm it differs from the true output."
- "Attempt B: show a bounded scan t in 0..20 in the ansatz r=B+p^k t fails to find the CRT match modulo q."
- "Attempt C: show partial information (only r \\equiv ((p-1)/2)! (mod p) and r^2 \\equiv -1 (mod q)) leaves multiple candidates for the output in {0,...,999}."

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independent reconstruction without modular inverses:
- Enumerate t in 0..q-1 in r=B+p^k t and find the unique t such that r \equiv A (mod q); confirm the resulting r matches the CRT reconstruction and yields the same r mod 1000.

task(s).name: novelty_equivalence_audit
task(s).reason:
Canonicalize and compare parent vs daughter defining constraint expressions using sympy:
- "Parent signature kernel: (q-1)!+1 together with (q-1)! and the modulus-coupling p^k q."
- "Daughter signature kernel: ((q-1)/2)!^2+1 together with x^2+1 and the modulus-coupling p^k q."
Canonicalization pipeline: simplify -> together -> cancel -> factor -> simplify, then compare srepr-normalized multisets.
Deterministic transforms tried: identity mapping and swap (p ↔ q).
Pass criterion: no transform makes the canonicalized constraint sets identical.

verification_notes: Computed k=v_13((41-1)!)=3, A ≡ 9 mod 41, and the unique Hensel lift B=239 modulo 2197.
CRT reconstruction gives r ≡ 61755 modulo 90077.

title: Iterated lift of a canonical square root of $-1$ to $(pq)^3$ and reduction mod $999$

identity.short_name: Iterated lift of a canonical square root of $-1$ to $(pq)^3$ and reduction mod $999$

problem_spec.summary: Choose the least positive square roots of $-1$ modulo two primes $p,q$, combine them via CRT to get $u$ modulo $N=pq$, then uniquely lift the congruence $u^2\equiv -1\pmod N$ to a congruence $w^2\equiv -1\pmod{N^3}$ within the class $w\equiv u\pmod N$, and output $w$ reduced modulo $999$.
givens: $p=5$ and $q=41$ are primes
$N=pq$
$a$ is the least positive integer with $a^2\equiv -1\pmod p$
$b$ is the least positive integer with $b^2\equiv -1\pmod q$
$u$ is the unique integer with $0\le u<N$, $u\equiv a\pmod p$, and $u\equiv b\pmod q$
$w$ is the least positive integer with $w\equiv u\pmod N$ and $w^2\equiv -1\pmod{N^3}$
$A$ is the unique integer with $0\le A<999$ and $A\equiv w\pmod{999}$
ask: Determine $A$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: ensures $x^2\equiv -1\pmod p$ is solvable in the general family

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: second prime factor for $N=pq$

parameter(s).name: lift_depth
parameter(s).purpose: target modulus exponent for lifting, from $N$ to $N^3$

parameter(s).name: output_modulus
parameter(s).purpose: final normalization modulus

invariant(s).name: two_roots_mod_prime
invariant(s).statement: For each prime $r\in\{p,q\}$, the congruence $x^2\equiv -1\pmod r$ has at most two solutions, and if solvable then the solutions are $\pm x_0$, so the least positive root is unique.

invariant(s).name: CRT_unique_u
invariant(s).statement: CRT gives a unique class $u\pmod N$ with $u\equiv a\pmod p$ and $u\equiv b\pmod q$, and $0\le u<N$ selects the integer $u$.

invariant(s).name: invertible_derivative_condition
invariant(s).statement: From $u^2\equiv -1\pmod N$ we get $\gcd(u,N)=1$, hence $\gcd(2u,N)=1$ since $N$ is odd.

invariant(s).name: lift_linearization_for_x2_plus_1
invariant(s).statement: Writing $y=x+tN^k$, the condition $y^2\equiv -1\pmod{N^{k+1}}$ reduces to a forced linear congruence $n+2xt\equiv 0\pmod N$, uniquely solvable when $\gcd(2x,N)=1$.

essential_assumption(s): $p,q$ are distinct odd primes with $p\equiv q\equiv 1\pmod 4$
The constraint $w\equiv u\pmod N$ is imposed to make the lift to $N^3$ unique
uniqueness_certificate(s): Least positive root modulo a prime removes the sign ambiguity in $x^2\equiv -1$.
CRT plus $0\le u<N$ gives a unique integer $u$.
Two successive forced linear congruences for the lift parameters give a unique class modulo $N^3$, and the least positive representative gives a unique $w$.
Reduction modulo $999$ gives a unique $A\in\{0,1,\dots,998\}$.

technique(s).name: sum_of_two_squares_to_root_minus_one
technique(s).purpose: produce a concrete solution to $x^2\equiv -1\pmod r$ via $x\equiv A\cdot B^{-1}$

technique(s).name: CRT_combination
technique(s).purpose: construct $u$ modulo $N=pq$

technique(s).name: iterated_linear_lift
technique(s).purpose: lift uniquely from modulus $N^k$ to $N^{k+1}$ by solving a forced linear congruence
outputs: explicit lift parameters and an explicit $w$ modulo $N^3$

technique(s).name: extended_euclidean_algorithm
technique(s).purpose: compute $a^{-1}\pmod N$
outputs: explicit modular inverses used in CRT and lifting

technique(s).name: canonical_residue_normalization
technique(s).purpose: return the unique residue in $[0,998]$

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: guarantees a unique residue class modulo $pq$ matching prescribed residues modulo $p$ and $q$

theorem(s).name: root_counting_in_field
theorem(s).role: a nonzero polynomial of degree $d$ over a field has at most $d$ roots; used to justify the two-root bound modulo primes

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Linearization: expanding $(x+tN^k)^2+1$ and forcing divisibility by $N^{k+1}$.

critical_trick(s): Fix local sign by taking least positive roots modulo $p$ and $q$.
Use CRT to assemble the unique $u$ modulo $N$.
Lift twice using a forced linear congruence modulo $N$ to reach modulus $N^3$.
Reduce modulo $999$ to normalize the output.

library(s).reason: reliable CRT and modular inverse computations and direct congruence verification

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd checks

task(s).name: correctness_verification
task(s).reason: Verify $a^2\equiv -1\pmod p$, $b^2\equiv -1\pmod q$, the CRT congruences for $u$, and the final congruence $w^2\equiv -1\pmod{N^3}$ with $w\equiv u\pmod N$, then compute $A=w\bmod 999$.

verification_notes: Least positive roots are 2 mod 5 and 9 mod 41, yielding u=132.
Two lift steps give w=1780557 modulo 8615125, so w mod 999 = 339.

title: Kummer carry valuation sets lift depth; Gaussian root with 2-adic sign; Hensel to cubes and CRT

identity.short_name: Kummer carry valuation sets lift depth; Gaussian root with 2-adic sign; Hensel to cubes and CRT

problem_spec.summary:
The lift depth k is defined by a p-adic valuation of a central binomial coefficient and computed via Kummer carry counting.
One local square root of -1 is seeded by a half-factorial (Wilson), another is constructed from a sum-of-two-squares representation (Gaussian integers),
with a 2-adic congruence selecting a canonical sign.
Both roots are Hensel-lifted to prime cubes and glued via CRT; the final output is the residue modulo 1000.
givens: Let $p$ and $q$ be distinct odd primes with $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ and $q\equiv 1\pmod{4}$.
Let $m=(q-1)/2$ and define $k=v_p\binom{q-1}{m}$ and $M=p^k q^3$.
Require that the base-$p$ addition $m+m=q-1$ has exactly three carries (so $k=3$).
Assume $q=a^2+b^2$ with $\gcd(a,q)=1$, and define $u_0\equiv b\,a^{-1}\pmod{q}$.
Define $u$ to be the unique element of $\{\pm u_0\}$ satisfying $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$.
Define $B$ as the unique integer with $0\le B<p^k$ such that $B\equiv ((p-1)/2)!\pmod{p}$ and $B^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^k}$.
Define $C$ as the unique integer with $0\le C<q^3$ such that $C\equiv u\pmod{q}$ and $C^2\equiv -1\pmod{q^3}$.
Define $r$ as the unique integer with $0\le r<M$ such that $r\equiv B\pmod{p^k}$ and $r\equiv C\pmod{q^3}$.
ask: Determine the unique $N\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ such that $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: provides the half-factorial seed modulo $p$ and the prime-power modulus $p^3$

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: provides the odd prime whose cube modulus supports a Hensel lift of $x^2\equiv -1$

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: participates in the Gaussian-integer construction of a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: participates in the Gaussian-integer construction of a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$

invariant(s).name: Kummer_carry_invariant_k_equals_3
invariant(s).statement: The number of base-$p$ carries in $m+m=q-1$ equals $k=v_p\binom{q-1}{m}$ and is fixed to be $3$.

invariant(s).name: half_Wilson_seed_root_mod_p
invariant(s).statement: For $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$ prime, $((p-1)/2)!^2\equiv -1\pmod{p}$, giving a simple root seed for $x^2+1$.

invariant(s).name: Gaussian_root_from_sum_of_two_squares
invariant(s).statement: If $q=a^2+b^2$ then $u_0\equiv b\,a^{-1}\pmod{q}$ satisfies $u_0^2\equiv -1\pmod{q}$; the sign is fixed by a 2-adic selector.

invariant(s).name: two_adic_sign_selector_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: The auxiliary congruence $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$ selects a unique sign among $\pm u_0\pmod{q}$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_unique_lifts_to_p_cubed_and_q_cubed
invariant(s).statement: For $f(x)=x^2+1$, each chosen seed is a simple root modulo the prime, hence lifts uniquely to $B\pmod{p^3}$ and $C\pmod{q^3}$.

invariant(s).name: bounded_quotient_controls_last_three_digits
invariant(s).statement: Writing $r=B+p^3 t$ with $0\le t<q^3$, the quotient $s$ in $p^3 t=(C-B)+q^3 s$ satisfies $0\le s<p^3$, enabling extraction of $t\bmod 8$ and hence $r\bmod 1000$.

invariant(s).name: CRT_gluing_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Since $\gcd(p^3,q^3)=1$, CRT gives a unique $r\pmod{p^3 q^3}$; the bound $0\le r<M$ fixes the integer.

essential_assumption(s): The carry-pattern condition enforces $k=3$ and sets the lift depth to $p^3$.
The data $q=a^2+b^2$ with $\gcd(a,q)=1$ ensures a computable root of $-1$ modulo $q$.
The 2-adic congruence $u\equiv 1\pmod{8}$ fixes a canonical sign.
Simple-root conditions ensure uniqueness of Hensel lifts for $x^2+1$.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
Kummer's theorem forces $k$ from the base-$p$ carry pattern.
The half-factorial residue fixes a seed modulo $p$, and simple-root Hensel lifting makes $B\pmod{p^3}$ unique.
The Gaussian construction produces $\pm u_0\pmod{q}$, and the 2-adic selector fixes $u$; Hensel then makes $C\pmod{q^3}$ unique.
CRT yields a unique $r\pmod{p^3 q^3}$ and $0\le r<M$ fixes $r$; reducing modulo $1000$ fixes $N$ uniquely.

technique(s).name: Kummer_carry_valuation_for_binomial
technique(s).purpose: compute a p-adic valuation by counting carries, fixing the prime-power lift depth

technique(s).name: Legendre_digit_sum_bridge
technique(s).purpose: derive the digit-sum form used to interpret carries

technique(s).name: half_Wilson_square_root_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: produce a canonical square root of $-1$ modulo $p$ using $((p-1)/2)!$

technique(s).name: Gaussian_root_constructor
technique(s).purpose: construct a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$ as $b\,a^{-1}$

technique(s).name: two_adic_sign_selector
technique(s).purpose: select a canonical root using an auxiliary congruence modulo $8$

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting_setup
inputs: $f(x)=x^2+1$ and a simple root modulo a prime
technique(s).purpose: guarantee existence and uniqueness of lifts to prime cubes

technique(s).name: bounded_quotient_trick_for_mod_1000
inputs: the identity $p^3 t=(C-B)+q^3 s$ with bounds on $t$
technique(s).purpose: avoid full CRT arithmetic by forcing $0\le s<p^3$ and extracting $t\bmod 8$

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_reconstruction
technique(s).purpose: glue local prime-power solutions into a global residue class
outputs: unique $r$ and hence unique normalized output $N$

technique(s).name: extended_Euclid_mod_inverse
technique(s).purpose: compute $a^{-1}\pmod{m}$

technique(s).name: mod_1000_normalization
technique(s).purpose: output the unique $N\in\{0,\dots,999\}$ with $N\equiv r\pmod{1000}$

theorem(s).role: supports the digit-sum bridge for Kummer carry counting

theorem(s).name: Kummer_theorem_carries
theorem(s).role: identifies $v_p\binom{n}{k}$ with base-$p$ carry count

theorem(s).name: Wilson_theorem
theorem(s).role: provides the half-factorial square-root identity

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root_lifting
theorem(s).role: provides unique lifting of a simple root to prime powers

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: provides existence and uniqueness modulo coprime moduli

theorem(s).name: Gaussian_integer_norm_trick
theorem(s).role: yields a square root of $-1$ modulo $q$ from $q=a^2+b^2$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Local-to-global reconstruction: build local roots (mod prime powers) and glue via CRT, with auxiliary congruences selecting canonical signs.

critical_trick(s): Use Kummer carry counting to force $k=3$ from a base-$5$ digit pattern.
Use $q=a^2+b^2$ plus a 2-adic condition to pin down a canonical root of $-1$ modulo $q$.
Use the bounded-quotient identity to extract $r\bmod 1000$ without computing the full CRT solution.

library(s).name: sympy

task(s).name: correctness_verification
task(s).reason:
Check:
$k=v_5(\binom{148}{74})=3$,
$B=57$ satisfies $B\equiv2\pmod{5}$ and $B^2\equiv-1\pmod{125}$,
$u=105$ satisfies $u^2\equiv-1\pmod{149}$ and $u\equiv1\pmod{8}$,
$C^2\equiv-1\pmod{149^3}$ and $C\equiv u\pmod{149}$,
CRT gives $r\equiv57\pmod{125}$ and $r\equiv C\pmod{149^3}$,
and $N=r\bmod 1000$ equals $932$.

verification_notes: Kummer/Legendre evaluation gives k=3; the lifted 5-adic root is B=57.
The unique root modulo 149 with residue 1 mod 8 is u=105, which lifts to C=2554859 modulo 3307949; CRT gives r ≡ 257266932.

title: $p$-adic valuation algebra (sum/product rules, compare $v_p$)

identity.short_name: $p$-adic valuation algebra (sum/product rules, compare $v_p$)

problem_spec.summary: Given exact powers of 2 dividing two monomials in integers x,y, determine the forced value of v_2 of a three-term expression built from x and y.
givens: $p=2$ is a prime and $v_2$ denotes the $2$-adic valuation.
$x,y\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $x\neq 0$ and $y\neq 0$.
Notation: $2^t\parallel n$ means $2^t\mid n$ but $2^{t+1}\nmid n$.
$2^{656}\parallel x^2y^3$.
$2^{639}\parallel x^5y$.
ask: Compute $v=v_2\bigl(x^5y^2+x^3y^4+x^{10}\bigr)$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: selects the valuation $v_p$

parameter(s).name: x
parameter(s).purpose: first integer input

parameter(s).name: y
parameter(s).purpose: second integer input

parameter(s).name: relation_mode
parameter(s).purpose: controls whether $v_p(x+y)$ is exactly determined or only lower-bounded

invariant(s).name: valuation_additivity
invariant(s).statement: For nonzero integers, $v_p(xy)=v_p(x)+v_p(y)$ and $v_p(x/y)=v_p(x)-v_p(y)$ when $y\mid x$ in $\mathbb{Z}_{(p)}$.

invariant(s).name: min_rule_for_sum_when_unequal
invariant(s).statement: If $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$ then $v_p(x+y)=\min\{v_p(x),v_p(y)\}$.

essential_assumption(s): In the default mode, the instance enforces $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$ so that $v_p(x+y)$ is uniquely determined.
When using the equal-valuation mode, the instance must provide enough auxiliary information (e.g., $v_p(x/p^t + y/p^t)$) if an exact value beyond the lower bound is desired.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Under the condition $v_p(x)\ne v_p(y)$, the valuation of the sum is forced to be the minimum, hence uniquely determined; $v_p$ itself is uniquely defined for each nonzero integer.

technique(s).name: valuation_vp_definition
technique(s).purpose: encode divisibility by prime powers as an exponent
outputs: the integer $v_p(n)$ such that $p^{v_p(n)}\mid n$ but $p^{v_p(n)+1}\nmid n$

technique(s).name: vp_product
technique(s).purpose: linearize multiplicative divisibility constraints

technique(s).name: vp_sum_exact_when_unequal
technique(s).purpose: compute $v_p(x+y)$ exactly

technique(s).name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
technique(s).purpose: compare valuations to deduce contradictions or to isolate unknown exponents
outputs: inequalities/equalities of integers coming from valuations

theorem(s).name: exact_division_arguments
theorem(s).role: translate "$p^t\mid n$" into "$v_p(n)\ge t$" and vice versa

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Divisibility by prime powers is controlled by a single integer exponent; valuation rules convert multiplicative structure into additive bookkeeping.

critical_trick(s): Factor out the smaller power of $p$ from a sum and use that $p$ does not divide the remaining unit-sum when valuations are unequal.
Use additivity of $v_p$ on products to convert given exact divisibilities into a solvable linear system for $v_p(x)$ and $v_p(y)$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic and sanity checks (e.g., gcd).

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: Small enumerations for preflight heuristics.

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: Randomized sampling for an independent verification route.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness + NT5 preflight:
  - Correctness: solve for (a,b)=(v2(x),v2(y)) from v2(x^2y^3)=656 and v2(x^5y)=639; compute v2 of each term in x^5y^2+x^3y^4+x^{10}; certify a unique minimum; conclude v2 of the sum.
  - NT5(a) small-mod-only: for mods in {2,3,4,5,7,8,9,11}, sampled many valid (x,y) and recorded the residue sets of S=x^5y^2+x^3y^4+x^{10} modulo each modulus; found S\equiv0 mod 2,4,8 always, but S mod 3/5/7/9/11 varies widely, so small-mod data cannot determine v2(S) (it only forces v2(S)\geq3).
  - NT5(b) tiny bounded scan: brute-forced |x|,|y|\leq200 for exact satisfaction of 2^{656}||x^2y^3 and 2^{639}||x^5y; found no solutions, so the answer cannot be guessed from tiny-range enumeration.
  - NT5(c) template fits: searched for simple affine templates α \cdot 656+β \cdot 639+γ with α,β\in[-3,3], γ\in[-50,50], and also common min/max/sum/diff/gcd/lcm combinations; none matched the true answer.
  - NT6 collision: the plausible wrong-branch value 907 differs from the true value.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent randomized check: construct many examples x=2^{97}u, y=2^{154}v with odd u,v, verify the givens by direct v2 computation, and compute v2(x^5y^2+x^3y^4+x^{10}) directly; confirm it is constant.

verification_notes: Solved v2(x)=a=97, v2(y)=b=154 from the linear system 2a+3b=656, 5a+b=639.
The three summand valuations are [793, 907, 970], with unique minimum 793.

problem_spec.summary: Let $p$ and $q$ be distinct odd primes and let $x,y$ be nonzero integers with $v_p(x)=v_p(y)$ and $v_q(x)

verification_notes: Computed r=u*v^(-1) mod 101^5 and found ord_101^5(r)=202.
Direct valuation check gives v101(x^(t/2)+y^(t/2)) + v17(x+y) = 3744.

title: Hensel-lifted i mod p^4 + mixed-order unit ratio; compute v_p(x^t-y^t)+v_q(x-y)+v_2(C(2t,t)) mod 1000

identity.short_name: Hensel-lifted i mod p^4 + mixed-order unit ratio; compute v_p(x^t-y^t)+v_q(x-y)+v_2(C(2t,t)) mod 1000

problem_spec.summary: Construct w as the Hensel lift of a square root of -1 modulo p^4 (forcing a 4-torsion Teichmueller component). Build x,y with equal p-adic valuation and define r\equivuv^{-1} (mod p^4). Use Teichmueller×principal-unit splitting to force t=ord_{p^4}(r)=4p^3, then compute v_p(x^t-y^t) via transfer+LTE, compute v_q(x-y) via the unequal-valuation min rule at q, and compute v_2(C(2t,t)) via Legendre/Kummer digit-sum. Output modulo 1000.
givens: $p,q$ are distinct odd primes with $p\equiv 1\pmod{4}$.
Choose $w_0$ with $w_0^2\equiv -1\pmod p$ and $p\nmid 2w_0$; let $w$ be the unique Hensel lift modulo $p^4$ with $w\equiv w_0\pmod p$ and $w^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^4}$.
Let $x=p^{a}(w\,q^{b}+p\,q^{c})$ and $y=p^{a}q^{b}$ with integers $a\ge 1$ and $b>c\ge 0$.
Define $r\in(\mathbb{Z}/p^{4}\mathbb{Z})^{\times}$ by $r\equiv uv^{-1}\pmod{p^{4}}$ after writing $x=p^a u$, $y=p^a v$ with $p\nmid uv$.
Assume $q\nmid p$ and $b>c$ so that $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$ (hence the min rule applies).
ask: Let $t:=\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(r)$. Compute $N:=v_p(x^t-y^t)+v_q(x-y)+v_2\binom{2t}{t}$.
Output the unique integer $N_0\in\{0,1,\dots,999\}$ such that $N_0\equiv N\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime for the p-adic valuation and modulus $p^4$; ensures -1 is a quadratic residue

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: secondary valuation via unequal-valuation min rule at q

parameter(s).name: w0
parameter(s).purpose: selects the Hensel lift branch

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: common p-adic valuation of x and y

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: controls the q-adic valuation of y

parameter(s).name: c
parameter(s).purpose: forces unequal q-valuations $v_q(x)=c$ and $v_q(y)=b$

invariant(s).name: hensel_lifted_i_root
invariant(s).statement: $w\equiv w_0\pmod p$ and $w^2\equiv -1\pmod{p^4}$; w is unique modulo $p^4$ (simple-root Hensel lift).

invariant(s).name: teichmueller_component_order_4
invariant(s).statement: $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(w)=4$.

invariant(s).name: equal_p_valuation
invariant(s).statement: $v_p(x)=v_p(y)=a$ and $x=p^a u$, $y=p^a v$ with $p\nmid uv$.

invariant(s).name: teichmueller_times_depth1_principal_unit
invariant(s).statement: With $s:=rw^{-1}$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/p^4\mathbb{Z})^{\times}$, one has $s\equiv 1\pmod p$ but $s\not\equiv 1\pmod{p^2}$, hence $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(s)=p^3$.

invariant(s).name: forced_mixed_order_4p3
invariant(s).statement: $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(r)=4p^3$.

invariant(s).name: unequal_q_valuations
invariant(s).statement: $v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$, hence $v_q(x-y)=\min\{v_q(x),v_q(y)\}$.

invariant(s).name: central_binomial_chain_invariant
invariant(s).statement: $v_2\binom{2t}{t}=s_2(t)$ where $s_2(t)$ is the number of 1s in the binary expansion of t.

essential_assumption(s): $p\equiv 1\pmod 4$ and $w_0^2\equiv -1\pmod p$ with $p\nmid 2w_0$, so Hensel lifting gives a unique $w\pmod{p^4}$.
$s=rw^{-1}$ is a nontrivial depth-1 principal unit (not $\equiv 1\pmod{p^2}$), forcing $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(s)=p^3$.
$p\nmid uv$, so LTE applies to $(u^4,v^4)$ at the prime p.
$v_q(x)\ne v_q(y)$, so the q-adic term $v_q(x-y)$ is rigid by the min rule.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Hensel rigidity: the simple-root Hensel lemma forces a unique $w\pmod{p^4}$ and hence forces $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(w)=4$.
Mixed-order rigidity: $r=w\cdot s$ with $\operatorname{ord}(w)=4$ and $\operatorname{ord}(s)=p^3$, and $\gcd(4,p)=1$, so $t=\operatorname{ord}(r)=4p^3$ is forced.
Valuation rigidity: transfer gives $v_p(u^4-v^4)=v_p(r^4-1)$, LTE gives $v_p(u^t-v^t)=v_p(u^4-v^4)+3$, and the unequal-valuation rule gives $v_q(x-y)$, so N is forced.
Binomial rigidity: Legendre/Kummer gives $v_2\binom{2t}{t}=s_2(t)$, which is uniquely determined once t is forced.

technique(s).name: valuation_vp_definition
technique(s).purpose: compute initial p- and q-adic valuations and identify unit parts

technique(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity_minus_one
technique(s).purpose: ensure solvability of $w_0^2\equiv -1\pmod p$ using $p\equiv 1\pmod 4$

technique(s).name: hensel_lifting_simple_root
technique(s).purpose: lift a simple root of $X^2+1$ from mod p to mod $p^4$ uniquely

technique(s).name: unit_factorization_x_equals_p_power_times_unit
technique(s).purpose: write $x=p^a u$, $y=p^a v$ with $p\nmid uv$

technique(s).name: modular_inverse_in_unit_group
technique(s).purpose: compute $v^{-1}\pmod{p^4}$ and form $r\equiv uv^{-1}$

technique(s).name: teichmueller_principal_unit_split
technique(s).purpose: factor r as $w\cdot s$ with $s\equiv 1\pmod p$ principal-unit part

technique(s).name: principal_unit_order_depth1_mod_p4
technique(s).purpose: show $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(s)=p^3$ when $s\equiv 1\pmod p$ but $s\not\equiv 1\pmod{p^2}$

technique(s).name: coprime_order_lcm

theorem(s).name: euler_criterion_minus_one
theorem(s).role: (-1/p)=(-1)^{(p-1)/2}; ensures -1 is a quadratic residue when p\equiv1 (mod 4).

theorem(s).name: teichmueller_unit_decomposition
theorem(s).role: supports splitting a unit modulo p^4 into torsion and principal-unit parts.

theorem(s).name: principal_unit_order_mod_p4_depth1
theorem(s).role: if $s\equiv 1\pmod p$ but $s\not\equiv 1\pmod{p^2}$, then $\operatorname{ord}_{p^4}(s)=p^3$.

theorem(s).name: LTE_for_u_n_minus_v_n
theorem(s).role: for odd p dividing U-V and p not dividing UV, $v_p(U^n-V^n)=v_p(U-V)+v_p(n)$.

theorem(s).name: min_valuation_rule_for_sum_or_difference
theorem(s).role: if $v_q(A)\ne v_q(B)$ then $v_q(A\pm B)=\min\{v_q(A),v_q(B)\}$.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: order decomposition in unit groups
p-adic valuation as a divisibility invariant
lifting roots via Hensel lemma

critical_trick(s): Hensel-lifted quadratic root gives a forced 4-torsion Teichmueller component w modulo p^4.
Depth-1 principal unit modulo p^4 has order p^3; coprime-order multiplication forces t=4p^3.
Transfer-and-LTE: move from $r^4-1$ to $u^4-v^4$, then apply LTE with exponent p^3.
Digit-sum evaluation: compute $v_2\binom{2t}{t}$ via Legendre/Kummer.

title: LTE-style exponent lifting for $v_p(a^n\pm b^n)$

identity.short_name: LTE-style exponent lifting for $v_p(a^n\pm b^n)$

problem_spec.summary: Given integers $a,b,n$ and a prime $p$ satisfying an LTE-admissible divisibility condition (e.g., $p\mid a-b$), compute the exact $p$-adic valuation of $a^n-b^n$ (and variants for sums of powers), thereby solving exponent-lifting divisibility questions.
givens: $p$ is a prime
$a,b\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $a\ne b$
$n\in\mathbb{N}$
LTE-admissibility constraints (variant-dependent), e.g. $p\mid (a-b)$ and $p\nmid ab$ for odd $p$ in the difference case; special handling when $p=2$.
ask: Compute $v = v_p(a^n-b^n)$ (or, in a sum-variant, $v=v_p(a^n+b^n)$) exactly in terms of $v_p(a-b)$, $v_p(a+b)$, and $v_p(n)$ as applicable.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: selects the valuation

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: first base in the power expression

parameter(s).name: b
parameter(s).purpose: second base (difference/sum pattern)

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: exponent to be lifted

parameter(s).name: sign_variant
parameter(s).purpose: selects which LTE formula applies

invariant(s).name: valuation_lift_formula
invariant(s).statement: In admissible cases, $v_p(a^n-b^n)$ decomposes as $v_p(a-b)+v_p(n)$ (with corrections in the $p=2$ and sum cases), reflecting exponent lifting.

invariant(s).name: unit_reduction
invariant(s).statement: After factoring out common $p$-powers from $a-b$ (or $a+b$), the remaining unit controls whether additional $p$-powers appear in the binomial expansion.

essential_assumption(s): The instance must satisfy the standard LTE preconditions (e.g., $p\mid a-b$, $p\nmid ab$ for odd $p$ in the difference case).
For $p=2$, the instance must specify parity constraints on $a,b$ and whether $n$ is even/odd to select the correct even-odd LTE branch.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Once LTE preconditions hold, the valuation $v_p(a^n\pm b^n)$ is determined by the relevant LTE formula, hence uniquely determined as an integer.

technique(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers
technique(s).purpose: compute $v_p(a^n-b^n)$ exactly via lifted exponent

technique(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_cases
technique(s).purpose: handle $a^n+b^n$ cases (often requiring $n$ odd)
outputs: exact valuation or reduction to difference case

technique(s).name: LTE_even_odd_cases
technique(s).purpose: apply the special 2-adic lifting formulas

technique(s).name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
technique(s).purpose: convert divisibility questions to inequalities in $t$

theorem(s).name: vp_sum_lower_bound
theorem(s).role: used after factoring to bound valuations and justify LTE steps

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Binomial expansion after extracting the maximal common $p$-power from $a-b$ (or $a+b$) reveals exactly how many additional $p$ factors appear as the exponent grows.

critical_trick(s): Factor $a-b$ (or $a+b$) as $p^t\cdot u$ with $p\nmid u$ and expand $(b+p^t u)^n$; show the first nonvanishing binomial term dictates $v_p(a^n-b^n)$.
Separate the $p=2$ case and track the extra $v_2(a+b)$ contribution when both bases are odd.

library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic (powers, modular exponentiation, and basic divisibility checks) for exact v2 verification.

verification_notes: Applied the 2-adic LTE even-exponent formula to x=2^30+1 and y=2^30-1.

title: LTE-style exponent lifting for $v_p(a^n\pm b^n)$ -- Buffed Seed

identity.short_name: LTE-style exponent lifting for $v_p(a^n\pm b^n)$ -- Buffed Seed

problem_spec.summary: Fix distinct odd primes $p,q$ and integers $A,B\ge 2$. Let $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and choose divisors $d_p\mid(p-1)$, $d_q\mid(q-1)$ with $d_p,d_q>1$ and cross-coprime to the other prime. Define $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)\,p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ and count the units $x\pmod M$ whose reductions modulo $p$ and $q$ have orders $d_p$ and $d_q$, and for which $x^n-1$ has exact $p$-adic depth $A$ and exact $q$-adic depth $B$. Report the count modulo $1000$.
givens: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes ($p\ge 3$, $q\ge 3$, $p\ne q$).
$A,B\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $A\ge 2$ and $B\ge 2$.
$d_p,d_q\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $1<d_q\mid(q-1)$, and additionally $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$.
Define the structured modulus $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$.
Define the exponent $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)\,p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$.
ask: Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of residue classes $x\pmod M$ such that the reduction of $x$ modulo $p$ has multiplicative order exactly $d_p$, the reduction modulo $q$ has multiplicative order exactly $d_q$, and simultaneously $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n-1)=B$ (where $v_r$ denotes the usual $r$-adic valuation). Determine $N=|S|$ and report the final answer as $N\bmod 1000$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: first prime controlling an order constraint (mod $p$) and a valuation-depth constraint (via $v_p$)

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: second prime; forces a genuinely two-prime valuation regime and prevents single-prime LTE templating

parameter(s).name: A
parameter(s).purpose: target exact $p$-adic depth $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and structural modulus exponent $p^{A+1}$

parameter(s).name: B
parameter(s).purpose: target exact $q$-adic depth $v_q(x^n-1)=B$ and structural modulus exponent $q^{B+1}$

parameter(s).name: d_p
parameter(s).purpose: prescribed nontrivial multiplicative order modulo $p$; the cross-condition $q\nmid d_p$ ensures $v_q(n/d_q)$ is controlled

parameter(s).name: d_q
parameter(s).purpose: prescribed nontrivial multiplicative order modulo $q$; the cross-condition $p\nmid d_q$ ensures $v_p(n/d_p)$ is controlled

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: structured exponent that couples ORDER information (via $d_p,d_q$) to VP constraints (via $p^{A-1},q^{B-1}$)

parameter(s).name: M
parameter(s).purpose: structured modulus with two prime-power components

invariant(s).name: anti_triviality_nontrivial_orders
invariant(s).statement: The constraints $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $1<d_q\mid(q-1)$ force $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p>1$ and $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=d_q>1$ for admissible $x$, hence $x\not\equiv 1\pmod p$ and $x\not\equiv 1\pmod q$ (blocking the direct one-step template on $x-1$).

invariant(s).name: anti_triviality_depth_positive
invariant(s).statement: $A,B\ge 2$, so the requested equalities $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n-1)=B$ are genuinely higher-depth lifting constraints and are not vacuous at the first prime layer.

invariant(s).name: cross_coprime_order_data
invariant(s).statement: The cross-conditions $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ ensure $\gcd(\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q),pq)=1$, so the $p$- and $q$-adic valuations of $n/d_p$ and $n/d_q$ are determined solely by the explicit factors $p^{A-1}$ and $q^{B-1}$.

invariant(s).name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
invariant(s).statement: With $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)\,p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ and the cross-coprimality constraints, one has $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$.

invariant(s).name: order_to_valuation_bridge_LTE
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$ then $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$ and (for odd $p$) LTE gives $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$; analogously, if $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=d_q$ then $v_q(x^n-1)=v_q(x^{d_q}-1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.

invariant(s).name: valuation_exactness_equivalence
invariant(s).statement: Under the structured exponent choice (so $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$), the exact-depth conditions $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n-1)=B$ are equivalent to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}-1)=1$ (equivalently $x^{d_p}\equiv 1\pmod p$ but $x^{d_p}\not\equiv 1\pmod{p^2}$, and similarly for $q$).

essential_assumption(s): All primes in the family are restricted to odd primes ($p,q\ge 3$), so no $2$-adic exceptional LTE branches are permitted in valid instances.

The cross-coprimality constraints $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ are part of admissibility; without them, $v_p(n/d_p)$ or $v_q(n/d_q)$ can pick up extra prime factors from $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)$ and the valuation targets cease to be sharp.

The order constraints enforce $x\not\equiv 1\pmod p$ and $x\not\equiv 1\pmod q$ for admissible $x$, explicitly ruling out the one-step template $v_r(x^n-1)=v_r(x-1)+v_r(n)$; the intended path must first reduce using multiplicative order (replace $x^n-1$ by $(x^{d_r})^{n/d_r}-1$) and only then apply LTE to obtain exact valuations.
uniqueness_certificate(s): For each admissible parameter tuple, the conditions define a finite set $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$. The order constraints contribute exactly $\varphi(d_p)$ choices modulo $p$ and $\varphi(d_q)$ choices modulo $q$. Using the ORDER->LTE bridge and the structured exponent invariants, the depth conditions are equivalent to excluding the unique Hensel lift of each root of $y^{d_r}\equiv 1\pmod r$ to a root modulo $r^2$ (for $r\in\{p,q\}$), yielding exactly $(r-1)r^{A_r-1}$ admissible lifts to $r^{A_r+1}$. Coprime prime powers combine independently, so $|S|$ is uniquely determined; the required output is the unique representative of $|S|\bmod 1000$ in $\{0,1,\dots,999\}$.

technique(s).name: order_then_LTE_bridge
inputs: structured exponent $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ together with $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$
technique(s).purpose: Use multiplicative order to rewrite $x^n-1$ as $(x^{d_p})^{n/d_p}-1$ (and similarly for $q$), then apply LTE to convert the order data into exact valuation equalities; this is the interaction kernel between ORDER and VP.

technique(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers
technique(s).purpose: Compute $v_r\big((x^{d_r})^m-1\big)$ exactly as $v_r(x^{d_r}-1)+v_r(m)$, enabling sharp depth constraints rather than mere lower bounds.
outputs: exact valuation formula isolating the condition $v_r(x^{d_r}-1)=1$ under the structured choice of $n$

technique(s).name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: Count residues modulo $r$ with multiplicative order exactly $d_r$ using cyclicity of $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$.

technique(s).name: valuation_filter_via_unique_root_lift
inputs: a residue class $x\pmod{r^{A_r+1}}$ whose reduction mod $r$ has order $d_r$
technique(s).purpose: Show that among the $r$ lifts of a fixed order-$d_r$ residue modulo $r$ to modulo $r^2$, exactly one satisfies $x^{d_r}\equiv 1\pmod{r^2}$ (Hensel uniqueness), hence the depth condition $v_r(x^{d_r}-1)=1$ excludes a single lift; then count free lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$.
outputs: lift-layer count factor $(r-1)r^{A_r-1}$ for each prime power

theorem(s).name: vp_sum_lower_bound
theorem(s).role: Supports the valuation bookkeeping underlying LTE and ensures the first nonvanishing term controls the exact valuation once order-reduction identifies the correct base.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic for prime $r$; used to count residues of exact order $d_r$.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime_power
theorem(s).role: For odd prime $r$, $(\mathbb{Z}/r^k\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; supports controlled lifting structure when enumerating residues modulo $r^{A_r+1}$.

theorem(s).name: hensel_unique_lift_for_simple_roots_of_y^d_minus_1
theorem(s).role: If $r\nmid d$ and $y_0^d\equiv 1\pmod r$, then $y_0$ is a simple root of $y^d-1$ modulo $r$ and lifts uniquely to a root modulo $r^2$; used to prove there is exactly one bad lift with $v_r(y^d-1)\ge 2$.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: For coprime moduli, gives a bijection between residues modulo the product and pairs of residues modulo each; used to combine the $13$- and $17$-counts.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Combine multiplicative-order reduction ($x^n-1=(x^{d})^{n/d}-1$) with valuation control via LTE to translate an order condition into an exact $r$-adic depth constraint.

critical_trick(s): Use $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$ and $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=d_q$ to force $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$ and $q\mid(x^{d_q}-1)$, then rewrite $x^n-1$ as $(x^{d_p})^{n/d_p}-1$ and $(x^{d_q})^{n/d_q}-1$ so that LTE applies.

Apply LTE together with $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ to deduce $v_p(x^n-1)=A \Longleftrightarrow v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^n-1)=B \Longleftrightarrow v_q(x^{d_q}-1)=1$, converting the valuation constraints into a precise lift-exclusion condition modulo $p^2$ and $q^2$.

Count admissible residues by (i) $\varphi(d_r)$ choices at the prime level, (ii) excluding the unique Hensel lift to $r^2$ and freely lifting to $r^{A_r+1}$ giving a factor $(r-1)r^{A_r-1}$ for each $r\in\{p,q\}$, and (iii) combining counts via CRT; then reduce the resulting integer modulo $1000$.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic (lcm, pow with modulus, gcd-free valuation logic).

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks:
- Brute-enumerated all units modulo $13^4$ and counted those with order $12$ mod $13$ and exact valuation $v_{13}(x^n-1)=3$; obtained $8112$.
- Brute-enumerated all units modulo $17^3$ and counted those with order $16$ mod $17$ and exact valuation $v_{17}(x^n-1)=2$; obtained $2176$.
- Multiplied the independent prime-power counts (CRT interface) to get $|S|=17651712$, hence $|S|\bmod 1000=712$.
NT5 preflight attempts:
(a) Small-mod-only attack: computed the residue modulo each $m\in\{2,3,4,5,7,8,9,11\}$ of the naive count ignoring the exact-depth filter, and compared it to the true residue; they already disagree for $m\in\{3,5,7,9,11\}$, so small-mod shortcuts cannot recover the final residue.
(b) Tiny bounded scan: counted solutions among the first $T\in\{1000,5000,20000\}$ integers viewed as residues modulo $M$ and extrapolated by scaling; the resulting estimates modulo $1000$ were $665,960,296$, none equal to $712$.
(c) Template resistance: checked that the final residue $712$ is not $0$ and is not a pure power of $2$, $3$, or $5$ modulo $1000$. Also tested several one-line shortcut residues obtained from the order-only lift count $|S|_{\text{naive}}$ by multiplying/dividing by factors in $\{13,17,12,16\}$ when integral (e.g., $|S|_{\text{naive}}\equiv 856$, $|S|_{\text{naive}}/13\equiv 912$, $|S|_{\text{naive}}/17\equiv 168$, $12\,|S|_{\text{naive}}\equiv 272$, $16\,|S|_{\text{naive}}\equiv 696$ modulo $1000$); none equals $712$.
NT6 collision resistance:
- Checked the plausible wrong-branch guess $|S|_{\text{naive}}=\varphi(12)13^3\cdot\varphi(16)17^2\equiv 856\not\equiv 712\pmod{1000}$.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independently verified the unique bad lift property:
- For each residue $y_0$ of exact order $12$ modulo $13$, enumerated the $13$ lifts to modulo $13^2$ and confirmed exactly one satisfies $y^{12}\equiv 1\pmod{13^2}$.
- For each residue $y_0$ of exact order $16$ modulo $17$, enumerated the $17$ lifts to modulo $17^2$ and confirmed exactly one satisfies $y^{16}\equiv 1\pmod{17^2}$.
This confirms the single-exclusion factor $(r-1)$ at the $r^2$ layer used in the count.

verification_notes: Brute-force local counts give 8112 admissible residues mod 13^4 and 2176 mod 17^3.
CRT independence yields |S|=17651712.

title: Mixed LTE count with an order-2 bridge and exact depths

identity.short_name: Mixed LTE count with an order-2 bridge and exact depths

problem_spec.summary: Two-prime counting problem where one prime uses a difference-of-powers valuation and the other uses a sum-of-powers valuation; order data forces the relevant base congruences and exact p-adic depths are enforced by excluding the unique Hensel lift at the prime-square layer.
givens: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes.
$A,B\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $A\ge 2$ and $B\ge 2$.
$d_p\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $1<d_p\mid(p-1)$ and $q\nmid d_p$.
$d_q\in\mathbb{Z}$ with $1<d_q$ and $2d_q\mid(q-1)$ and $p\nmid d_q$.
Parity guard: $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd.
Define $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ and $n=\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)\,p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$.
ask: Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of residue classes $x\pmod M$ such that $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, and simultaneously $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$. Determine $|S|$ and output the unique integer $0\le N<1000$ with $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime at which a difference-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: prime at which a sum-of-powers LTE constraint is imposed

parameter(s).name: A
parameter(s).purpose: target exact depth $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and modulus exponent $p^{A+1}$

parameter(s).name: B
parameter(s).purpose: target exact depth $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ and modulus exponent $q^{B+1}$

parameter(s).name: d_p
parameter(s).purpose: prescribed nontrivial order modulo $p$; keeps $v_q(n/d_q)$ sharp

parameter(s).name: d_q
parameter(s).purpose: half the prescribed even order modulo $q$ (order $2d_q$); keeps $v_p(n/d_p)$ sharp

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: structured exponent coupling the order constraints to exact valuation depths, and (via the parity guard) enabling LTE for a sum of powers

parameter(s).name: M
parameter(s).purpose: structured two-prime modulus for independent prime-power lifting and CRT recombination

invariant(s).name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ and hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$, so $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.

invariant(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
invariant(s).statement: The guard that $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd implies $n/d_q$ is odd (since $p^{A-1}q^{B-1}$ is odd), enabling the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.

invariant(s).name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
invariant(s).statement: Under $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$, one has $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$.

invariant(s).name: mixed_order_to_LTE_bridge
invariant(s).statement: For admissible $x$, $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$ gives $p\mid(x^{d_p}-1)$ and yields (LTE) $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$; and $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ gives $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$ and (with $n/d_q$ odd) yields $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$.

invariant(s).name: exact_depth_equivalence
invariant(s).statement: Using the structured exponent valuations, the targets $v_p(x^n-1)=A$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=B$ are equivalent to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.

essential_assumption(s): All primes are odd; no 2-adic exceptional LTE branches occur.
Cross-coprimality guards $q\nmid d_p$ and $p\nmid d_q$ ensure $v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(n/d_q)$ are exactly $A-1$ and $B-1$ with no extra prime factors leaking in from $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)$.
Parity guard: $n/d_q$ is odd (equivalently $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ odd), required for the sum-of-powers LTE identity at $q$.
The order constraint at $q$ is even ($\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$) so that $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$; without this, $v_q(x^n+1)$ would not be controlled by LTE in the intended way.
uniqueness_certificate(s): For each admissible parameter tuple, the constraints reduce (via LTE bridges) to exact valuation conditions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$. For each residue of order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and each residue of order $2d_q$ modulo $q$, Hensel's lemma for simple roots implies there is exactly one lift to the prime-square layer with valuation $\ge 2$ for the relevant polynomial ($y^{d_p}-1$ at $p$, $y^{d_q}+1$ at $q$), hence exactly $p-1$ (resp. $q-1$) admissible lifts with valuation $1$. All higher lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ are free and contribute a factor $r^{A_r-1}$. The $p$- and $q$-conditions are independent and recombine uniquely by CRT, so $|S|$ (hence $|S|\bmod 1000$) is uniquely determined.

technique(s).name: order_count_in_cyclic_group_mod_prime
technique(s).purpose: Count residues of exact order $d_p$ modulo $p$ and exact order $2d_q$ modulo $q$ using cyclicity of unit groups modulo primes.

technique(s).name: order2_to_negative_one_bridge
technique(s).purpose: Convert an even order condition into the congruence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ needed for sum-of-powers LTE.

technique(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
technique(s).purpose: Ensure $n/d_q$ is odd so the odd-exponent LTE sum formula applies.

technique(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers
technique(s).purpose: Compute $v_p(x^n-1)$ exactly after order reduction to a difference of powers.

technique(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_odd_exponent
technique(s).purpose: Compute $v_q(x^n+1)$ exactly after order reduction to a sum of powers.

technique(s).name: structured_exponent_vp_targets
technique(s).purpose: Extract the fixed values $v_p(n/d_p)=A-1$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-1$ under the cross-coprimality guards.

technique(s).name: compare_vp_on_both_sides
technique(s).purpose: Turn target depths $A,B$ into prime-square exclusion statements on $x^{d_p}-1$ and $x^{d_q}+1$.

technique(s).name: hensel_filter_for_exact_depth
inputs: polynomials $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$ with simple roots modulo primes
technique(s).purpose: Show that among the $r$ lifts of a fixed root modulo $r$ to modulo $r^2$, exactly one lift makes the polynomial divisible by $r^2$; excluding it forces valuation exactly $1$.
outputs: exactly $p-1$ good lifts at the $p^2$ layer per order-$d_p$ residue
exactly $q-1$ good lifts at the $q^2$ layer per order-$2d_q$ residue

technique(s).name: mod_prime_power_lifting_count
technique(s).purpose: Count free lifts from $r^2$ to $r^{A_r+1}$ and ensure the valuation decision persists under lifting.

technique(s).name: CRT_product_count
technique(s).purpose: Recombine prime-power solutions into solutions modulo $M=p^{A+1}q^{B+1}$ via CRT bijection.

theorem(s).name: cyclicity_of_units_mod_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: $(\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z})^\times$ is cyclic; enables exact-order counting via Euler totient.

theorem(s).name: unique_element_of_order_2_in_cyclic_group
theorem(s).role: A cyclic group has a unique element of order $2$; in $(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ it is $-1$, giving $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ when $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$.

theorem(s).name: vp_sum_lower_bound
theorem(s).role: Valuation bookkeeping used inside LTE arguments to isolate the first nonvanishing term.

theorem(s).name: LTE_difference_of_powers_for_odd_prime
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a-b)$ and $r\nmid ab$, then $v_r(a^m-b^m)=v_r(a-b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: LTE_sum_of_powers_for_odd_prime_odd_exponent
theorem(s).role: If odd prime $r\mid(a+b)$, $r\nmid ab$, and $m$ is odd, then $v_r(a^m+b^m)=v_r(a+b)+v_r(m)$.

theorem(s).name: hensel_unique_lift_for_simple_roots
theorem(s).role: Simple roots of a polynomial modulo an odd prime lift uniquely to roots modulo the prime square; used for both $y^{d_p}-1$ and $y^{d_q}+1$.

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem_coprime_moduli
theorem(s).role: Provides a bijection between residues modulo a product of coprime moduli and pairs of residues modulo each factor.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Translate multiplicative-order information into divisibility of $x^d\pm 1$, then use LTE (difference and sum) to obtain exact p-adic depths and count solutions by lifting across prime powers.

critical_trick(s): Use the even order condition $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ to force $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ (unique order-2 element) and combine with a parity gate so that LTE applies to the sum $x^n+1$.
Apply LTE separately to $x^n-1$ at $p$ and to $x^n+1$ at $q$ to convert target depths into the prime-square exclusions $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=1$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=1$.
Count by excluding the unique Hensel lift at each prime-square layer and then freely lifting to higher prime powers, and recombine counts via CRT.
